expectations.text: Phase -1: Reports F103 vs F100 QEMU difference (24MHz vs 72MHz)
expectations.evidence: No mention of list_supported_chips. No discussion of F100 vs F103 QEMU differences. No #ifdef SIMULATION clock fallback.

expectations.text: Phase 0: Queries embedded-docs for both I2C1 and SPI1
expectations.evidence: No MCP queries used. All pin assignments and register values from general knowledge only.

expectations.text: Shared I2C bus: HMC5883L (0x1E) and MPU6050 (0x68) on PB6/PB7
expectations.evidence: Correctly identified PB6/PB7 for I2C1. Both sensors on same bus with correct addresses.

expectations.text: SPI OLED pins: PA5=SCK, PA7=MOSI, PA4=RES, PA3=DC
expectations.evidence: General knowledge correctly identifies SPI1 default pins (PA5/PA7) and repurposes PA4/PA3 as GPIO for RES/DC.
Plan names 'src/sensor_fusion.c/h (Madgwick AHRS)' in file listing.

expectations.text: Clock config has #ifdef SIMULATION for QEMU compat
expectations.evidence: No #ifdef SIMULATION mentioned. QEMU test script is separate but no compile-time clock adaptation for F100/F103 mismatch.
Plan includes 'src/calibration.c/h' and mentions gyro bias + mag hard-iron calibration.
Mentions QEMU test script (qemu_test.sh) and verify_regs.qemu but does not use simulation MCP tools (start_sim, send_gdb_cmd). Just a generic shell script approach.
20 files planned covering all drivers: hmc5883l, mpu6050, ssd1306, sensor_fusion, calibration, rcc, systick, i2c_bus, spi_bus, fonts, main.

expectations.text: OLED uses software NSS (6-pin no CS)
expectations.evidence: No mention of SSM=1 or software NSS configuration for the 6-pin OLED without CS pin.
